I’ll make a small continuation bet of ~1/3 pot (200) to fold out unpaired hands and exploit Flash’s short stack while keeping my risk controlled. Plan: fold to a large raise; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and only continue with real equity (e.g. a club or a jack) or check to control the pot.

Facing a 200 bet into an 800 pot on a dry Q-6-2 rainbow board with T9o, which has zero equity—no pair, no draws, pure air. Minni's button min-raise preflop and continuation bet after two checks is consistent with a wide value range (Qx, overpairs) or bluffs, but calling requires realizing equity later OOP multiway with a short-stacked BB who can call light. Pot odds are 4:1 but irrelevant without showdown value or fold equity; folding preserves stack for premium spots in this tournament phase. Consistent with preflop and flop plan to check-fold aggression.

On a flop of Qd-6h-2c, I have 8-high with no straight or flush draws and no overcards. Minni, the pre-flop aggressor, has bet 200 into a pot of 600 after Elon folded. With only 8-high and no real equity to improve to a winning hand, calling or raising would be a waste of chips. The most disciplined and correct play is to fold.

Pot is 150 from blinds, to call 100, min-raise 200. This indicates SB (Flash) folded their 50, BB (Minni) checked, folded to button. Excellent steal opportunity on the button with dead SB money. 86o is a profitable open-raising hand here due to position, playability (straight potential, can flop strong hands), and deep stacks. Opponents aggressive historically but BB checks multiway often. Standard raise to 3x BB (300 total) to build pot and take initiative. Stealing blinds frequently maximizes EV over tournament.

I am in the Small Blind with 8s 6d, which is a very weak hand to play out of position against a raise from the Button (Elon). Elon has shown significant aggression in previous hands (Hand #15 and Hand #16), and my stack is relatively short (44-big blinds). Calling with this hand would put me in a difficult spot post-flop without the initiative. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my chips for a more favorable starting hand.

Elon (Button) opened to 300; I’m in the big blind with 2♣5♥. Pot is 450 and I must call 200 more. 2-5 offsuit is a very weak, often-dominated hand and I’ll be out of position postflop against a Button opener. Calling requires ~30.8% equity to break even; given the wide but still aggressive opening range and deep stacks, this hand has poor playability and will put me in many marginal spots. I have a healthy stack (142.5 BB) so there’s no need to gamble here — fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with 8c 2h, which is one of the weakest starting hands in Texas Hold'em. While the button is a great position to steal blinds, 82o is too weak to justify an open, especially with a stack of 44 big blinds where I need to be more calculated with my risk-taking. Minni and Elon have significantly larger stacks and have been active, so I will wait for a better opportunity.

Q5s is a marginal hand but heads-up it's playable. I already have the 50 small blind posted and the pot is 150; calling 50 more (100 total committed) gives ~3:1 pot odds to see a flop and I have positional advantage postflop (button acts last). A min-raise to 200 could be used as a steal, but Elon in the big blind will defend widely and my offsuit queen-five is often dominated — I don't want to inflate the pot out of position without a stronger holding. I'll complete to see the flop and re-evaluate.

Preflop in the big blind with T♥8♦ offsuit against a small blind limp from Minni after the button folds. The pot is 200 with no money to call, providing excellent implied odds to realize equity on a flop with a speculative hand like T8o. Raising is unnecessary as this hand lacks strength for value and is not a great bluffing candidate given Minni's history of aggression. Folding is -EV with dead money in the pot. Checking keeps the pot multiway potential low (heads-up) and sees a flop cheaply, where we can play postflop based on board texture and villain tendencies. Deep stacks (my 21k, Minni 14k) support realizing equity profitably over the long tournament.

Check. With Qh5s on a 6s Td 9s flop I have only one overcard and a backdoor spade/straight possibility — essentially no made hand and very little equity versus a reasonable calling range. The flop is fairly connected and two-spaded, so many of Elon's holdings (pairs, broadway cards, spade draws) dominate me or have good equity. I’m on the button (act last), so I prefer pot control: check to see his reaction rather than inflating the pot with a marginal hand. Plan: fold to a large bet or a check-raise, call modest continuation bets (roughly 1/3 pot or smaller) to realize equity and see a turn, and only start committing chips if I pair my Queen or pick up a real draw.

Flop board 6s Td 9s gives me top pair (Th pairing Td) with a weak 8 kicker and a gutshot straight draw (needing a 7 for 7-8-9-T). The board has a spade flush draw and possible straight draws (e.g., 7-J, Q-J). Minni limped from SB (weak wide range: low pairs, suited connectors, Ax) and checked flop, representing weakness or a draw. Deep stacks (21k effective) favor betting for thin value against her worse pairs (e.g., 66, 99 unlikely given limp-check, weaker like A-high, low pairs, flush/straight draws) while protecting vs. her draws and charging them. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs. Half-to-2/3 pot bet (150 into 200) balances value/protection, denies equity, and sets up fold equity postflop given her history of aggression but passive limp-check here.

I checked the flop with Qh5s to keep the pot small with a marginal holding. Elon c-bets 150 into the ~200 preflop pot (pot now 350) — about a 75% pot bet. My hand has only one overcard (Q) and a runner-runner spade possibility, so very little showdown equity versus his likely c-bet range (T,9,6, pocket pairs, broadways and spade combos). To call 150 into 350 I need ~30% equity (150/(350+150)=30%), which I don’t have. Raising would be a thin bluff with limited fold equity and would commit chips out of position with a weak hand. With deep stacks and many better spots ahead, the correct play is to fold and conserve chips.

J♣3♠ is a weak offsuit hand, but I'm on the button in a 3‑handed spot — ideal for stealing the blinds. Pot is only 150 and Flash (BB) is relatively short (~43BB) so a small-ish ergonomic open will fold out a lot of marginal BB defenses and keep the pot manageable if Elon (deep SB) decides to 3‑bet. I’ll raise to a standard 3xBB (300) to take the initiative and preserve fold equity; if Elon 3‑bets big I’ll let this go, if I get called I’ll c‑bet favorable textures and proceed cautiously postflop knowing my hand has limited showdown value.

T3 suited is a weak speculative hand, particularly out of position in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). Minni's range from button is wide but still includes many strong hands that dominate this. Post-flop play would be difficult OOP with reverse implied odds if hitting two-pair or lower straights/flushes. Flash in BB may defend, but no value in calling 250 into 450 pot with this hand. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots in a tournament setting.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Ten offsuit (ATo), which is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. Minni opened for 3x from the Button. While I could 3-bet for value and protection, my current stack is 4,300 (43 BBs), and calling is a solid, lower-variance play that allows me to see a flop. Given Minni's aggressive history, calling keeps the pot manageable while still playing a hand that is likely ahead of her opening range.

I have flopped trips with the best possible kicker (Ace). This is a monster hand. In this situation, checking to the pre-flop aggressor (Minni) is the standard and most profitable play. Minni has shown significant aggression in previous hands, so there is a high probability she will continuation-bet with a wide range of hands, including bluffs and air. By checking, I allow her to commit more chips to the pot. If she bets, I will likely just call to keep her range wide and trap her, or consider a check-raise if the board texture changes on the turn. Leading out (donk-betting) here might cause her to fold her weaker hands immediately.

I missed the flop with J♣3♠, but I have position and the initiative from my preflop raise. The board T♠ 8♦ T♥ is paired and can favor the caller’s range, yet Flash checked — giving me a chance to take it down now. I’ll make a half‑pot continuation bet (325) to apply pressure on overcards and weaker pairs while keeping my commitment reasonable against Flash’s ~4,100 stack. If he shoves or raises large I’ll fold (J3 has almost no showdown value vs aggression); if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn, often checking back marginal hands and folding to significant pressure. This size balances fold equity and risk in a 3‑handed spot.

I have flopped trips with an Ace kicker (Td Ah on a Ts-8d-Th board), which is an extremely strong hand. Minni has bet 325 into a 650 pot. By calling rather than raising, I maintain a deceptive line and allow Minni to continue bluffing or value-betting with a wider range of hands on the turn. Raising here might scare off her air or medium-strength hands (like pocket pairs or an 8), whereas calling keeps the pot manageable and maximizes value from her potential aggression in later streets.

I have trips with the best possible kicker (Ace). The turn King is a potential scare card but also a card that might have hit Minni's range if she has Kx. Since I have been check-calling to trap her aggressive style, I will continue with a check on the turn. This allows her to continue bluffing or to bet for value if she improved to a pair of Kings. Leading out now might reveal the strength of my hand and cause her to fold her air.

I check. J♣3♠ has essentially no showdown value on T♠ 8♦ T♥ K♣ — I missed the flop and the King doesn’t help me. I c‑bet the flop as a standard continuation, but Flash’s call narrows his range toward at least a pair (T, 8, or a pocket pair) or a deliberate float. With Flash very short (~3.8k), any bet here risks inducing a check‑raise shove or a call from hands that already beat me; I don’t want to build a pot with junk. Checking controls the pot size, keeps options open, and lets me fold to meaningful aggression. If he checks back I can reevaluate the river; if he bets I’ll fold to significant pressure.

I have successfully improved to a full house, Tens full of Aces (Td-Ts-Th-As-Ah). This is an incredibly strong hand. The board is Ts-8d-Th-Kc-As. Minni checked back the turn, which suggests she might have a medium-strength hand like a King or an Ace that she was pot-controlling, or perhaps she was giving up on a bluff. Now that the Ace has hit the river, she is very likely to call a value bet if she has any Ace or even a King. While AA or KK would beat me, her check on the turn makes those hands less likely, and I must value bet here. A bet of around 60% of the pot (800) is likely to be called by her range while maximizing my return.

Fold. With J♣3♠ on T♠‑8♦‑T♥‑K♣‑A♠ I have essentially no showdown value. Flash called my flop c‑bet and checked the turn, then leads the river for 800 into a 2100 pot — a line much more consistent with at least a pair (T or 8) or a stronger value holding (boat/quads) than with frequent bluffs. Calling 800 to win ~2900 requires ≈27.6% equity; J3 has virtually none against his likely calling range. Raising is reckless: it risks an immediate shove from a short stack or building a pot with no equity. Preserve the stack and fold to this river bet.

Js5c is a trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or strong playability. Even on the button in a 3-handed pot preflop, it lacks the equity or fold equity to justify entering the pot. Opponents Flash (SB, 50BB) and Minni (BB, 134BB) have shown aggression in past hands but also folding tendencies. Deep stacked at 214BB, no need to speculate with marginal holdings early in the tournament (hand 21/150). Folding preserves stack for better spots.

I am in the Small Blind with Jd 2c. Elon has folded on the Button. This is a blind-vs-blind situation against Minni in the Big Blind. J2o is a very weak hand, and although it's tempting to complete the blind or raise to steal, Minni has shown significant aggression in previous hands. Given my current stack size of 5025 (roughly 50 BBs), there is no reason to play such a marginal hand out of position. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with K8o in a 3-handed game. This is a standard opening hand for a button raise. My goal is to steal the blinds or play the hand with initiative and position if called. A standard 3x raise to 300 is appropriate given the stack depths and table dynamics.

I’m in the small blind with Kc6d facing a 3bb button open to 300 (pot 450, to-call 250). K6o is a weak, easily-dominated hand with poor playability out of position — I’d need roughly 250 into a 700 pot (~36% equity) just to break even, and that’s before considering the big blind’s decision. A 3-bet bluff could isolate but leaves me OOP vs the button and exposes me to 4-bets or cold-call scenarios I don’t want with this holding. With deep-ish stacks and only marginal upside, preserving chips for better spots is the right play here.

3-handed, in BB facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot is 450, calling 200 more for pot odds of roughly 3:1 (needing ~25% equity). 9d5d is suited with straight and flush potential, solid defensive hand against a wide button opening range which likely includes many weaker hands. Folding is too tight 3-handed; raising is unnecessary as the hand plays well postflop multiway or heads-up. Will call and see a flop, leveraging position and implied odds with deep stacks (effective 47BB).

Flop: 7c-8d-Jc with 5d9d gives a gutshot straight draw (4 outs to any 6 for 5-6-7-8-9), backdoor diamond flush draw (3 diamonds total), and minor wheel draw potential. No pair or strong equity. Heads-up OOP vs. button raiser Flash, who has a wide continuing range including Ax, broadway, suited connectors, pairs. Pot 650, acting first. Checking realizes equity: free card if checked behind (unlikely but possible), or pot odds to call a standard c-bet (~33-50% pot) needing ~25% equity which we approximate vs. polarized range. Deep stacks (Flash 47BB), implied odds for runner-runner. Betting risks check-raise from better hands or folding equity loss against calls; too thin for value, too weak for bluff. Check to control pot and play post-flop.

I have flopped middle pair (Eights) with a King kicker on a wet board (7c-8d-Jc). My opponent Elon has checked to me. While checking back is a viable option to control the pot, this board is quite coordinated with several straight and flush draws. I will bet around half the pot to extract value from draws (like T9, 69, or clubs) and smaller pairs (like 7x or lower pocket pairs), while also protecting my hand against overcards. A bet of 350 into the 650 pot is a standard continuation bet size in this spot.

Facing a 350 bet into 1000 pot on a coordinated flop (7c8dJc) OOP with 5d9d, giving a gutshot straight draw (4 outs to 6), backdoor diamond flush draw, and dynamic potential. Pot odds are excellent (~3.8:1, requiring ~21% equity), and vs. Flash's wide button continuation range (Ax, broadway, suited connectors, small pairs, bluffs), we have sufficient equity (~25-30% estimated via quick range vs. range approximation) including fold equity realization and runner-runner outs. Folding is too tight given odds and position dynamics 3-handed (now heads-up). Raising as a bluff squeeze risks commitment OOP with marginal equity and Flash's 43BB stack allowing calls from better; better to control pot size, induce bluffs, and reassess turn. Deep effective stacks support calling for implied odds if we hit. Standard defensive float/call.

Turn board adds 2h, a relatively blank low card that doesn't significantly improve my hand or complete obvious draws beyond the existing gutshot straight draw (still 4 outs to any 6 for 5-6-7-8-9 straight). No pair, no flush draw progress, just ~8-9% equity to river plus backdoor potential. OOP vs. aggressive button Flash who raised wide preflop (3x) and c-bet flop on draw-heavy 7c8dJc (~50% pot), continuing a polarized range of value (pairs like JJ, 88, 77, Jx, sets, some straights like T9) and bluffs/draws (suited connectors, Ax suited). Pot 1350 (~13.5BB), effective stack vs. Flash only 4375 (~43BB), so committed somewhat but room for maneuver. Acting first: betting risks check-raise from better hands (e.g., Jx, two-pair), thin value calls, or folds from worse while bloating pot OOP with weak hand; fold equity questionable as Flash has shown aggression and position. Checking best realizes equity: free river if checked behind (possible with missed draws), induces bluffs from his wide range, controls pot size, and preserves stack. Excellent implied odds unnecessary since direct outs suffice vs. pot odds if facing a bet (~25%+ equity threshold for half-pot bet, which gutshot + fold equity approximates vs. his continuing range). Long-term: avoid spew OOP with marginal draw in tournament spot 3-handed early (hand 22/150), prioritize survival and fold equity realization post-check.

The turn 2h is a blank that doesn't significantly change the board texture. I have middle pair (Eights) with a King kicker, which has decent showdown value but is not strong enough to value bet for three streets. By checking back the turn, I control the pot size and can more easily call a reasonably sized bet on the river if a draw misses, or simply check down to showdown. Betting here might isolate me against better hands like a Jack or two-pair, while checking keeps Elon's bluffing range and weaker pairs in the hand.

River Kd completes a diamond flush draw (benefiting hands like AdXd, KdXd, suited connectors with diamonds) and potentially broadway draws (QJT for straight), but blanks my gutshot straight draw (no 6) and backdoor potential from 5d9d, leaving complete air with ~0 direct showdown equity. Board: 7c8dJc2hKd is draw-heavy overall (straights like T9, 56; flushes clubs/diamonds; pairs/two-pair/set value). OOP vs. Flash's button range (wide preflop raise: Ax suited/off, broadway, suited connectors, small-medium pairs), who c-bet flop ~50% pot (polarized: value JJ/88/77/Jx/sets/T9; semi-bluffs/draws) then checked turn (2h blank-ish, suggesting missed draws, medium strength like Jx/top-pair, pocket pairs below J, or traps). Pot 1350 (~13.5BB), my stack 20k+ deep, Flash 4375 (~43BB effective). Acting first on river: Betting as bluff risks bloating pot OOP with no equity (Flash calls Jx/A-high/better bluffs or check-raises value); fold equity low vs. his position/aggression history (frequent preflop raises, c-bets). Checking maximizes EV: induces bluffs from missed draws/A-high (e.g., AQo, KQs), checks behind showdown value, controls pot size OOP early tournament (hand 22/150, 3-handed preserve stack). No value to bet, no fold needed. Standard check with air OOP river after check-check turn realizing equity passively.

I have improved to two pair (Kings and Eights) on the river. The board texture (7c-8d-Jc-2h-Kd) is somewhat coordinated, but Elon's check on the river following a check on the turn suggests he likely doesn't have a powerhouse hand like a set or a better two-pair (like KJ). My check on the turn likely induced Elon to put me on a missed draw or a marginal hand. By betting now, I can extract value from any Jack-x hands (AJ, QJ, JT, J9) that Elon might have called with on the flop and will now feel compelled to call a single river bet. A bet of 800 into the 1350 pot is a standard value sizing that targets these medium-strength hands.

River Kd completes diamond flushes (relevant since 8d on board and my 5d9d missed everything) and broadway straight draws (Q10), while blanking my gutshot. Pure air: 0% showdown equity, no draws left. Pot 2150, facing 800 bet (~37% pot) from aggressive button Flash (3575 behind, ~36BB), giving ~2.7:1 odds (need ~27% equity to call profitably, ignoring future play). His line (preflop raise wide, flop c-bet drawy board, turn check after my call suggesting possible medium strength or missed draw, river bet) is consistent with value (KJ/KQ/Jx/AJ/top pair improved, two-pair, sets, straights like T9/QT, flush) over pure bluffs (missed draws less likely to bet river after turn check). Bluff range thinner here OOP facing my check (Ax high, missed clubs unlikely to value bet). No implied odds (river), fold equity only via raise but jamming 3575+ into deep stack risks vs. calling range without blockers. Early tournament (hand 22/150, 3-handed play), deep effective stacks (mine 207BB), prioritize folding marginal/zero equity spots to avoid spew OOP, preserve stack for premium hands. Standard tight fold realizes EV positively long-term vs. aggressive opponent.

9♠4♦ is a weak holding, but on the button in a 3-handed game I should be opening a wide range to apply pressure and use position. Flash (BB) is relatively short-stacked and Elon (SB) is deep — a standard exploitative open to ~3x the big blind denies the BB good odds to see a flop and generates fold equity. I’ll raise to 300 total. Plan: fold to a sizeable 3-bet or a shove from Flash (I won’t call off with this hand); if called I’ll c-bet favorable boards and use position to fold when irrelevant.

A8s suited in SB facing a button raise from Minni, who has shown aggression in raises throughout history. Button opening range is wide, and As8s has excellent blockers to strong hands like AA, AK, AQ, and great playability with flush and straight potential. Position is OOP but pot odds are great (250 to call into 450+). However, 3-betting polarizes our range, applies fold equity against Minni's wide range, and isolates vs BB if called. Raising to 900 (3x) balances value and bluff, denies equity to worse aces and suited connectors. Flash BB is shorter stack but may fold or shove, which we can handle with our deep stack. Calling OOP risks multiway pot without initiative; folding too tight.

I am facing a raise from Minni on the button and a 3-bet from Elon in the small blind. While Queen-Six of spades is a suited hand, it is far too weak to continue against a 3-bet, especially when I would be out of position against Elon for the remainder of the hand. Folding is the standard play to preserve my stack.

I opened a wide range from the button, but facing Elon's 3-bet to 900 my 9♠4♦ is far too weak to continue. Calling 600 into a 1,300 pot gives poor equity against a typical SB 3-bet range and leaves me with bad implied/reverse implied odds — any pair I hit is likely to be dominated or difficult to realize value against a deeper stack. A bluff 4-bet would be ill-advised here against a deep, capable opponent who can easily 5-bet or call and outplay me postflop. Preserve 13k+ chips and wait for a much better spot in position or a cheaper steal attempt.

Preflop on the button with 6c4c suited connector. SB Flash likely folded (dead money), BB Minni checked, showing weakness with a wide range. Excellent position heads-up. 64s is a strong speculative hand with good playability, straight/flush potential, and suited for postflop. Raising to steal the 150 dead pot (SB + BB) + apply pressure on BB's weak checking range. Standard 3x raise size to 300 given blinds 50/100. Deep stacks allow this; folding or limping too passive for tournament stack maximization.

I am in the Small Blind with 8-7 offsuit. Elon, the button, has opened for 3x (300). While the pot odds for a call are somewhat tempting (250 to win 450), 8-7o is a weak hand that plays poorly out of position, especially against an aggressive button and with a potentially squeeze-happy Big Blind (Minni) yet to act. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Fold. Elon opened to 3bb from the button and I’m in the big blind with K♦3♣ — a very weak offsuit K with poor kicker and little post‑flop playability out of position. Calling 200 to see a flop will leave me dominated by a large portion of his opening range and create difficult OOP decisions; 3-betting as a bluff is thin (no strong blockers) and likely to be called or 4-bet. With deep stacks but early in the tournament, preserving chips and waiting for a better spot is the higher EV line.

I am on the button with 9-4 suited. While suited cards have some value in a 3-handed game, 9-4 is generally too weak to open, especially since my stack is significantly smaller than both Minni's and Elon's. Both blinds have shown a willingness to 3-bet or play aggressively post-flop. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity to commit my chips.

Heads-up as the small blind (button) with 4h5s — a small, connected offsuit hand that plays reasonably well in position. Pot is 150 and I only need to put 50 more to see a flop, but a min-raise to 200 applies pressure and picks up the blind sometimes while keeping the pot manageable if called. Against Elon (who has shown an active preflop range) I expect a lot of calls, so I’ll keep the raise small to preserve implied odds and exploit position postflop. If he 3-bets big I’ll fold this marginal hand; if called I’ll use position to C-bet favorable flops or check and control the pot on danger boards.

KJo suited (KhJh) is a premium hand facing a min-raise from the SB after the BTN folds. Minni's SB opening range is extremely wide, including many weaker kings, suited hands, broadways, and trash that she raises light. KJ blocks strong hands like AK/AQ/KQ and has excellent equity (often 40%+) against her range. Deep stacks allow for post-flop playability, but 3-betting takes the initiative, builds the pot when ahead, denies equity to her calling range, and can win the pot immediately ~40-50% of the time preflop. Standard 3-bet size around 3x her raise (600 total) balances value, semi-bluff potential if she shoves wide hands, and fold equity. Calling is fine but passive; folding is -EV. Raising larger risks overcommitting OOP, smaller lacks fold equity.

I opened to 200 as a small isolation/steal, but Elon 3-bet to 600. I have 4♥5♠ — a weak, low offsuit connector with limited equity versus a 3-bet range of pairs and broadways. Calling 400 to see a flop gives ~3:1 pot odds (need ~25% equity), which this hand usually doesn't have vs his likely range, and I’ll face difficult postflop decisions and reverse implied odds when I hit a small pair. 4-betting to 1000 as a bluff is too risky without blockers or fold equity. Preserve my stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

I’m on the button with Qc7c facing a small open to 200 (100 to call). Q7s is a playable hand in position: decent flop playability, backdoor club/straight potential, and good post‑flop maneuverability against an opening range that is likely wide from the SB. With deep stacks (I have ~129 BB) I prefer to keep the pot controllable and realize equity in position rather than thin 3‑bet bluff or fold out equity. The BB is relatively short and may fold or call — calling keeps our options open and lets us exploit post‑flop. I will call the 100 (total commitment to 200) and play the flop in position.

4h8s is a complete trash hand: offsuit, gapped connectors with low cards, minimal straight or flush potential. Currently in SB facing what appears to be an unraised pot with button limp (Minni called 100), pot 250 to call 50 more (already posted SB 50, total call to 100). This gives direct pot odds of about 5:1 (~16.7% equity needed), but multi-way OOP from SB with this hand, my equity is likely below that against any reasonable calling range from BB and button. Post-flop playability is poor; rarely strong, often dominated. Raising has no fold equity with this hand and commits more chips OOP. Folding the dead SB money is standard; deep stack (214BB), wait for premium hands or better stealing spots to maximize stack long-term.

I have King-Queen offsuit, which is a strong hand in this situation. Minni limped from the button and Elon folded his small blind. I want to raise here to build a pot and take the initiative, as KQo is well ahead of a typical button limping range. A raise to 400 (4x the big blind) is a standard play to put pressure on the limper and potentially end the hand immediately or play a larger pot with a range advantage.

Call — put 400 total in. I’m on the button with Qc7c and I already called the opener; Flash’s re‑raise to 400 makes the pot ~550 and I only have to call 300 more (pot after my call ≈ 850), so I need ~35% equity to break even. Q7s has good playability and backdoor club/straight potential, and being in position lets me control the pot and exploit post‑flop. Flash’s sizing and stack (~52BB) look like a relatively wide/aggressive range rather than a pure value shove, so a call is a reasonable, low‑risk way to realize equity. Min‑raising/3‑betting here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and can get punished by shoves or calls from stronger hands, so I’ll call and evaluate the flop — continue aggressively with strong/top or drawy flops, fold or proceed cautiously on dry, hostile boards.

As the pre-flop aggressor, the Ace on the flop is much better for my perceived range than for Minni's limp-calling range. I will place a standard continuation bet of approximately 35% of the pot to represent the Ace and attempt to take the pot down immediately. If I am called, I will likely evaluate the turn and potentially check-fold as I have very little equity to improve against an Ace.

Board: As 5h 3d. I have Qc7c — no pair but two clubs (backdoor flush), and a decent second-high card. Flash's small bet (300 into a 1150 pot) gives me ~21% break-even equity to call. Against his likely wide/aggro continuation range I have enough equity and strong post‑flop position to realize it. Raising with this marginal hand would bloat the pot and risk a shove from Flash (he's ~48 BB) so I prefer to keep the pot controllable. Plan: call the 300 to see the turn, fold to heavy turn aggression unless I pair up or pick up a club or clear improvement.

I am out of position against Minni on a board that is heavily weighted toward my perceived range but one where she has already called a continuation bet on the flop. The Ace-high flop is generally good for the pre-flop aggressor, but by calling the flop, Minni indicates she likely has a piece of the board, such as an Ace, a five, or a straight draw. The 6d on the turn doesn't improve my King-high, and I have very little equity to improve to the best hand. Against a player who has shown willingness to call, a second barrel is unlikely to generate enough folds to be profitable. I will check and evaluate her action, likely folding to a bet as I have no showdown value and no draws.

Check. On As-5h-3d-6d I haven’t improved with Qc7c — no pair, no live flush or meaningful straight draw (the backdoor club flush is effectively dead with only one card to come). I have ~6 outs to a pair on the river (~12–13% equity), which isn’t worth bloating the pot now. Flash checked the turn after a flop c-bet; that line can indicate either pot‑control with a made Ace/pair or a missed c‑bet. Betting here as a bluff risks a shove or a call from better hands and needlessly ramps the pot with very little equity. In position it’s better to control the pot and see the river: if I pair (Q or 7) I can value-bet or call reasonable bets; if the river bricks and he barrels big, I’ll fold. So I’ll check and keep the pot small.

I have King-high on a board of As-5h-3d-6d-Jd. My hand has no showdown value, as almost any hand Minni called with on the flop (Aces, pocket pairs, or even a 5) currently beats me. While I represented a strong range pre-flop and on the flop, checking the turn likely signaled weakness. The Jack on the river doesn't improve my hand, and although it could potentially be a scare card, Minni has shown a willingness to call in previous hands. A bluff here is risky given the pot size and her likely holding of at least a pair. I will check and hope to see a cheap showdown, though I expect to fold if she bets.

The river (As 5h 3d 6d Jd) leaves me with Qc7c — no pair, no club flush, and the river actually completes diamond draws. Flash’s line (preflop raise, flop c‑bet, then checking turn and river) reads like pot‑control with a made hand or missed draws rather than a definite river bluff. Betting into him here has very little fold equity versus hands that beat me (A, J, any pair) and risks getting called or shoved; I have no showdown value to turn into a credible bluff. Best play is to keep the pot small: check and go to showdown (or fold to a river bet).

3-handed on the button with Th3h suited, which has decent playability for flush and gutshot potential. Facing a small to-call of 100 into a 150 pot (excellent implied odds if called), likely from a limped or min-raised blind with wide range. Standard to raise wide from button in short-handed play to steal dead money or isolate. Opponents have folded frequently to aggression in history (e.g., multiple folds to 3bets/raises). Raising to 400 (4x the to-call amount) applies pressure, builds pot when ahead, and denies equity. Deep stacks allow postflop maneuvering in position.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Deuce of hearts. Elon has raised to 400 from the Button, which is a 4x big blind open. While the hand is suited, Q2 is quite weak to play out of position against an aggressive button raiser, and there is still the Big Blind (Minni) to act behind me. Calling 350 more into a 550 pot with this hand is generally a losing play in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Facing a button open to 400 (4x the BB) with J♦3♣ in the big blind, I fold. J3o is a weak, easily dominated hand with poor playability out of position; calling 300 to chase a pot that would become 850 requires roughly 35% equity which I don’t have vs a reasonable button opening range. A 3-bet would be a pure bluff and risks a larger 3-bet or commitment OOP. Preserve the stack and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with King-Trey offsuit. The action is folded to me. This is a weak starting hand, but in a 3-handed game, any King has some value for steal potential. However, both Minni and Elon have shown willingness to defend or 3-bet aggressively in previous hands. Given my current stack size and the fact that I'm against two much larger stacks, I will choose to fold this weak hand and wait for a better spot rather than risking chips on a marginal steal attempt.

Heads-up on the button (small blind) with 7♣9♥ — a playable hand but marginal to build a big pot with. Pot is 150 and it costs only 50 more to see the flop; calling keeps me in position postflop (big advantage) and gives excellent pot odds to realize equity. Raising here risks getting 3‑bet or bloating the pot out of speculative holdings against Elon, who has shown aggression. I’ll complete the call to see a flop and use position to control the pot and evaluate further.

Pocket 3s in BB vs SB limp after BTN fold. Heads-up pot of 200 with deep stacks (215BB effective). Small pocket pair has excellent implied odds for set-mining and plays well multi-way or heads-up post-flop. SB limp range is wide and weak, but checking realizes equity cheaply without risking fold equity unnecessarily early in tournament. No need to raise a speculative hand here; prefer seeing flop in position for free.

I have middle pair (7s) with a 9 kicker on 8♥-7♦-2♦ and I'm out of position versus Elon (big blind). This is a marginal made hand that wants to control the pot and gather information rather than bloating the pot OOP. Elon defended preflop and can have a wide range (overcards, 8x, pocket pairs, and diamond draws). I’ll check to keep the pot small, induce bluffs, and call reasonable-sized bets on the flop to see the turn. Plan: check-call small to medium bets (keep showdown value), fold to very large/overbearing aggression unless there are clear read/runner improvements on later streets, and if he checks back I’ll re-evaluate the turn for a modest value or protection bet.

Pocket 3s on 872dd flop in BB vs SB check after limp. Heads-up, pot 200, deep effective stacks (~120BB for Minni). My underpair is likely best hand vs SB's wide limping/checking range (Ax high, suited broadways, weak pairs, draws). Board has diamond flush draw and some straight draws (65, T9), so betting denies equity and protects. SB check suggests weakness, no strong hands. Small bet sizes thin value from worse (worse pairs, Ax) and folds out pure air. 125 into 200 (~60% pot) is good size for value/protection without overbetting marginal made hand. Checking risks giving free cards to draws/overcards that can improve. Betting realizes EV better long-term.

I have middle pair (7♣) with a 9 kicker on 8♥-7♦-2♦. Elon bet 125 into a 325 pot — calling 125 would make the pot 450, so I need ~27.8% equity to break even. In position against a fairly wide C-bet/continuation range he defended preflop with, my pair has reasonable showdown value and likely meets or exceeds that threshold. There are two diamonds on board so I must respect diamond draws, but I have no redraws and raising here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand. I’ll call to use position, keep the pot controlled, and re-evaluate on the turn — folding to heavy turn aggression unless I improve or pick up a strong read; if checked to on the turn I may probe with a small value/protection bet on safe cards.

I improved to two pair on the turn (9s pairing my 9 with my 7) and Elon checked to me. This is a clear value/protection spot — I'm ahead of most of his missed overcards and single-pair hands (8x, 9x, 2x) and I want to charge diamond draws and worse pairs while keeping the pot controlled. A roughly half‑pot bet will get called by worse hands and draws but won't fold out too many thin callers; it also keeps the river maneuverable. If he check‑raises big I'll re‑evaluate (that often represents a set or a very strong hand and I'll proceed cautiously). Bet ~1/2 pot to extract value and protect.

Pocket 3s on 872dd/9s board in BB vs SB limper who check-called flop c-bet and now leads 225 into 450 pot (half-pot bet). Effective stacks deep (116BB for Minni). My underpair to all board cards is vulnerable but has good showdown value vs her wide SB limping/check-call range (Ax, weak pairs, diamonds flush/straight draws like 65dd/T10, floats). The 9s turn completes T10 straights and adds gutshots/spade draws, but her lead could be value (87/98/T9/89/JT pairs/top pair), semi-bluff draw, or thin value/overpair. Folding loses to pure air/bluffs/draws we beat; raising 33 lacks strong value/protection and risks stacking off behind. Call gets pot odds (~4:1 effective, 20% equity needed; 33 realizes ~35%+ vs calling range) and keeps pot small for river control/cheap showdown. Re-evaluate river: if checked through or small bet, we can call/value; barreling villain likely weak. Long-term EV positive in deep tournament spot.

River paired the board (2c) which is very dangerous for my two‑pair. Any deuce in Elon’s range is now trips and pocket 9s/7s (or pocket 8s) beat me as well. Elon’s line (flop bet, check on turn, call my turn bet) contains a mix of worse hands I can extract from (single pair 8x, some 9x) and hands that now crush me (2x, pocket 9/7/8). Betting here would thin‑value a few worse hands but will also get called or raised by the hands that beat me; there’s little fold equity and I don’t want to inflate a pot where I’m vulnerable. Pot is small relative to stacks, so pot‑control and taking it to showdown is the prudent EV+ line. I’ll check and see a showdown.

River pairs the deuces (2c pairing 2d), transforming my pocket 3s into threes full of twos—a monster hand heads-up. Villain's SB limp-check-call flop c-bet (likely draws, Ax, small pairs, weak broadway)-turn donk bet (possibly hitting 9x, straight 56/T10, or semi-bluff)-river check line suggests medium strength at best: top pair (8x/9x), weaker two pairs, trips with 2x, missed draws, or thin value. Unlikely to have better full house (77/88/99/22) given passive preflop limp and check. Her check misses nuts/opportunity to bet value/protect. Pot 900, deep effective stacks (~116BB villain), value bet 2/3 pot (600) targets calls from TP/TK, 2x trips, missed draws that showdown, 98/T9 etc., while denying equity and setting up fold equity if raised. Checking forgoes value from calling range and risks split pot with bluffs. Long-term +EV max extraction.

River: I have two pair (9s & 7s) on 8♣7♦2♦9♠2♣ — a strong but vulnerable hand (any deuce, pocket 9s or pocket 7s beat me). Elon’s line (flop c‑bet, check on the turn, call my turn bet, then a sizable river stab) is consistent with both value hands and some thin/turn-callers, but the river pairing to a deuce reduced most bluff/fold combos. The pot is 1,500 and it costs 600 to call, so I need ~29% equity to break even — reasonable for two pair heads‑up vs his likely calling range (lots of single pairs and missed draws). Raising to 1,200 bloats the pot with a vulnerable hand and will fold out hands I want value from or get me in trouble vs true value (trips/quads). Folding is too tight given the pot odds and my showdown value. I’ll call 600 and take it to showdown.

I have 8♠2♦ — a garbage holding, but I'm on the button in a 3-handed game so position and fold equity are significant. I want to seize the initiative and try to take the blinds now rather than passively calling and playing a difficult post‑flop hand. I will open-raise to 300 (≈3x the BB): large enough to apply pressure and pick up dead money, but small relative to effective stacks so I can fold to a big 3‑bet or shove without risking a large portion of my stack. If faced with a shove/large 3‑bet from Elon or Flash I'll fold; if called I'll use position to c‑bet favorable flops and otherwise fold to strong resistance. The objective is steady chip accumulation and avoiding marginal, high-variance spots with a near‑worthless hand.

82o is one of the worst possible hands: offsuit, disconnected, low cards with no high card strength or straight/flush potential. Facing a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive but whose range is still far stronger than this holding (likely including all pairs, broadways, suited connectors, etc.). SB defense charts suggest folding this hand wide against button opens. Pot odds are not compelling (calling 250 into 450 pot ~36% equity needed, but we have minimal equity vs villain's range). Deep stack play favors folding trash preflop to preserve chips for premium spots in this tournament. No need to speculate.

I am in the Big Blind with Kd 6h. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. In a 3-handed game, King-high hands have significant defensive value against a Button opening range. I am getting 3.25:1 odds to call (200 to win a pot that will be 650). While K6o is a marginal hand and I will be out of position, the pot odds are sufficient to justify a call and see a flop, looking to hit a King or a favorable board texture.

I am out of position on an Ace-high board (8h-Ac-Td) that strongly favors Minni's pre-flop button opening range. With only King-high and no significant draw, I have very little equity in this pot. The standard play here is to check and likely fold to any continuation bet from the opponent, as my hand is too weak to continue against aggression on this texture.

I raised preflop and picked up a pair on the flop (8♠ on 8♥ A♣ T♦). As the preflop aggressor in position versus one caller this is a good spot to continuation‑bet: I have showdown value, can fold out many overcards and unpaired holdings, and charge Ax/Tx and backdoor draws. Pot is 650, so a half‑to‑two‑thirds pot c‑bet applies pressure but keeps the pot manageable if I get resistance. My plan: bet to take it down or get a cheap read — if Flash raises I will fold most of the time because my kicker is poor and I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position. If called I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and re‑evaluate.

I have King-high with no pair and no real draws on an Ace-high board (8h-Ac-Td). This board is much better for Minni's pre-flop button opening range than for my big blind defending range. Minni has made a continuation bet of 400 into a pot of 650. Given I have no equity and am out of position, there is no reason to continue in this hand. I will fold.

3-handed on the button with 8d9d suited connectors, excellent speculative hand with great position. SB limped and BB checked, showing weakness. Pot is 150, to call 100 gives direct odds but implied odds are huge with deep stacks (my 205BB, opponents 59BB and 134BB). Raising to 300 (3x BB) is standard here: it can steal the blinds frequently, isolate weaker ranges, and build the pot while maintaining fold equity. If called, 89dd plays well postflop in position multiway or heads-up. Folding or limping is too passive; calling is okay but raising has better EV long-term by denying equity and controlling pot size.

I am in the Small Blind with 7h 4h. Elon, the button, has raised to 300. While suited, 74s is quite weak and I will be out of position for the remainder of the hand. Given my stack size of approximately 60 big blinds and the fact that Minni in the Big Blind has shown aggression previously, I don't want to commit chips with such a marginal hand from a disadvantageous position.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with A♦T♥ — a hand worth defending but easily dominated by stronger A‑x and broadway hands. Effective stacks are deep (~134bb), so I don’t want to commit or inflate the pot OOP with a marginal hand. A min‑3bet to 500 is too small to achieve fold equity and just widens his calling range; a large 3bet here would be overly polarized. Calling the extra 200 (300 total) gives me ~30.8% break‑even equity to see a flop and keeps many of his weaker hands in the pot. Postflop I’ll play cautiously OOP: value-bet or protect when I pair the Ace, otherwise check/fold to significant continue bets unless I pick up a strong draw or can exploit a predictable bet sizing.

Elon c-bet 300 into a 650 pot on a very dry 6♠-5♣-2♦ board. I have A♦T♥ — decent showdown value (top card Ace) but easily dominated by stronger A‑x and broadway hands. The bet is ~half‑pot; calling 300 commits me 300 to win a pot of 1,250 (post‑call), so my break‑even equity is only ~24%. Against a wide continuation‑bet range my Ace‑high has enough equity to justify a call, and I don’t want to inflate the pot OOP with a marginal hand by 3‑betting. I’ll call to realize equity, keep the pot controllable, and re‑evaluate on the turn: if I pair the Ace I can extract value; if I miss and face heavy pressure I can fold.

Heads-up on the flop with position after Minni checks. My 89dd has a gutshot straight draw (needing a 7 for the wheel to Broadway-ish straight: actually 5-6-7-8-9), backdoor diamond flush draw, and two overcards. Equity is decent (~20-30% vs calling range), but more importantly, as the preflop raiser, this low connected board hits my button opening range well (pairs, sets, overpairs, draws). Minni's BB defending range is wide and capped somewhat after checking. C-betting ~60% pot denies equity to her floats/draws, builds pot with my equity, and has fold equity against her weak holdings. Checking risks giving free cards to her draws or letting her realize equity. Deep stacks allow for implied odds if I hit, but betting now maximizes EV by charging her and maintaining initiative. Size to 400: balanced, gets calls from weaker pairs/draws, folds worse.

Call 400. I defended the BB preflop with A♦T♥ and the flop 6♠-5♣-2♦ is very dry — it misses a lot of the raiser’s value hands and he’s likely c-betting a wide range. The pot is 1050 and calling 400 commits me to a 1450 pot, so I need ~27.6% equity to justify a call. My Ace‑high has reasonable showdown value vs many missed hands and there’s still ~6.4% to hit an Ace on the turn (~12.5% by river). Raising OOP with a marginal hand is risky (little fold equity and vulnerable to reraises) and folding would be too tight given the pot odds. I’ll call to realize equity, keep the pot controllable, and fold to significant turn aggression unless I pair the Ace or pick up clear additional equity.

I have A♦T♥ on 6♠-5♣-2♦-9♠ — currently just Ace-high and no meaningful draw. I defended preflop and called the flop to keep the pot controllable; the turn 9♠ didn’t improve me and only leaves 6 outs (3 A, 3 T) to pair on the river (6/46 ≈ 13%). Pot is 1,450 and I’m out of position, so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal hand. Checking controls the size of the pot and lets Elon barrel missed hands. If he checks behind we go to showdown with the best reasonable holding; if he bets I’ll fold to large, polarized bets but call smaller bets that offer correct pot odds or look like bluffs. If I hit an Ace or Ten on the river I can extract value; otherwise I’ll avoid getting committed OOP.

Heads-up on the turn with top pair (nines) and a decent kicker (eight), plus a backdoor diamond flush draw. The board is 6s-5c-2d-9s, which is fairly dry but connects with straight draws (e.g., 3-4, 7-T) and flush draws now possible with spades. Minni's BB defending range after limp-calling preflop and calling flop is wide: capped hands like 77, 88, low pairs, Ax suited, gutshots (34,47), and some bluffs/floats. Her check-check sequence suggests weakness, but she could have showdown value or draws. With deep stacks (198BB vs her 128BB) and position, betting for value is optimal: charges draws, gets calls from worse pairs (e.g., 77, 88, 55/66/22 trapped), extracts from overcards/A-high. Checking risks giving a free river to her equity or letting her bluff. Size to ~2/3 pot (1000 into 1450) balances value/protection, polarizes range, and maintains fold equity vs pure air while inviting calls from ~1/2 equity hands. Pot control unnecessary with strong hand and initiative.

Fold. Elon’s line (open preflop, c‑bet on a very dry 6♠‑5♣‑2♦ board, then a sizable turn barrel to 1,000) is consistent with a value‑heavy range (9x, sets, two‑pair, overpairs) and is unlikely to be overcome by Ace‑high. Calling 1,000 into a 2,450 pot (you’d be fighting for 3,450) requires ~29% equity; I only have ~6 outs to pair (3 A, 3 T) — ~13% to improve — and my current showdown equity versus his turn‑betting range is likely well below break‑even. Raising OOP as a bluff with no pair or real turn equity is too risky. Preserve a healthy stack for later, fold this marginal Ace‑high spot.

I am on the button with Ace-Nine offsuit (Ad 9c), which is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. My stack is currently the smallest at 5925 chips, so I need to look for opportunities to build it up. In this position, I will raise to take control of the pot and potentially take down the blinds immediately. A standard 3x raise is 300.

Holding K♦ T♥ in the small blind vs a button open to 300 is worthwhile to see a flop but not strong enough to 3-bet out of position. Calling costs 250 more (300 total) into a 450 pot — roughly 35.7% break-even — and keeps the pot manageable while preserving stack depth against possible squeezes from the big blind. I plan to see the flop and continue if I hit a pair, good kicker, or a strong draw; otherwise fold to heavy post‑flop aggression. A min‑raise to 500 would unnecessarily commit more chips OOP and invite 4‑bets, so I’ll just call.

Pocket deuces in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 and a small blind call. Excellent pot odds to call 200 into 700 (over 3:1 direct odds), and deep stacks (Flash 56BB, Minni 125BB, me 211BB) provide massive implied odds for set mining. Button raiser (Flash) is aggressive and likely wide-ranged from late position based on history. I have position postflop. Folding is too tight; 3-betting risks playing OOP with a marginal hand multiway. Calling to see flop and hit a set or improve.

I flopped top pair (T) with the best possible kicker (K) on 9♥‑3♥‑T♠ in a 3‑way pot. I’m out of position vs the preflop raiser (Flash) and the big blind (Elon), so I want to both extract value from worse tens/medium pairs and deny free cards to heart/straight backdoors. A half‑pot bet charges draws and gets called by thin value hands while keeping the pot controllable OOP. I’ll lead for value/protection and re‑evaluate if faced with significant aggression.

Pocket 22 set-mined pre-flop successfully due to great odds and implied odds with deep stacks. However, flop 9h 3h Ts completely misses: underpair with no kicker value, vulnerable to any overpair, any pair on board, flush/straight draws which are abundant (hearts flush draw, KJ/QJ/JQ straight draws). Minni's SB lead (1/3 pot bet) after calling button open likely polarized: strong made hands (99/TT/39/T9 sets/two-pair/top pair good) or draws/bluffs exploiting multiway pot. Pot odds ~4:1 but equity <20% against her value range, and Flash (aggro button) behind can pile on with better draws/strength. Continuing risks spew OOP next, no fold equity for raise (blocks nothing useful). Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I have middle pair (Nines) with the top kicker (Ace) on a board of 10-9-3 with a heart draw. Minni has led out for half the pot (450 into 900) after calling pre-flop. This 'donk bet' could represent a Ten, a draw (like hearts or a gutshot), or a weaker Nine. Given that I have a strong kicker and the pot odds are 3:1, folding would be too tight. However, raising might only isolate me against better hands like a Ten or a set. Calling is the best play to keep the pot controlled and see how the turn develops.

Turn pairing the King gives me two pair (K-T). Flash called my flop c-bet, so his range is wide: Tx, 9x/3x, heart draws and some Kx or pocket pairs. The hands that legitimately beat me are mainly sets (TT/99/33) and made two-heart flushes; lots of his call range is still behind. I’m out of position so I should continue for value and to charge hands that can call (worse tens, single-pair hands, Kx) rather than checking and allowing a free river. A ~2/3-pot bet (~1,200 into 1,800) extracts value from worse hands, prices some turn/river antics, and keeps the pot reasonable OOP. If Flash raises big I’ll re-evaluate (large aggression often denotes a flush or a very narrow value range).

The King of hearts is a very unfavorable card for my hand. It completes the heart flush draw and introduces an overcard to my pair of Nines. Minni's decision to lead out on the flop (a 'donk bet') and then fire a large second barrel (1200 into 1800) on the turn indicates significant strength, likely a flush, a King-x hand that just improved, or a set. I only have middle pair with no heart in my hand, meaning I have no redraws to a flush and very few outs to improve. Given the board texture and the betting action, my hand is likely crushed, and calling here would leave me with a short stack facing another potentially large bet on the river. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my remaining chips.

On the Button in a 3-handed game I should open a wide range to steal the blinds and play postflop in position. 7♦9♥ (7h9d) isn’t premium but is playable in position — decent straight/backdoor equity and lots of ways to realize equity versus a single opponent. Raising to 300 (≈3x the big blind) is a standard, pressure-raising size here: it forces folds from the shorter-stacked big blind (Flash) often and either isolates Elon (SB) or takes the pot immediately. Limping is too passive and invites uncomfortable spots; if Elon 3-bets big I can fold, if he calls or Flash defends I’ll play the flop in position and keep pot control.

Preflop from SB facing a button min-raise to 300 (3x BB) from Minni, who appears aggressive and opens wide from late position based on hand history (frequent raises and light postflop action). ATo offsuit is a strong defending hand: premium ace blocker, good equity vs wide BTN range (~40-45% equity vs typical 35% open). OOP postflop is a concern, but 3-betting isolates vs BB (shorter stack Flash may fold), applies pressure to Minni's wide range, and builds pot with strong hand. Calling risks multiway pot OOP and gives Flash squeeze opportunity. Folding too tight. Standard 3bet size OOP vs raise: ~3x villain's raise to 900 total for balanced sizing. Pot will be 50(SB)+100(BB)+300(Minni)+900=1350, good SPR control. Deep stacks allow maneuvering postflop if called.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Queen suited, a premium hand in 3-handed play. Minni (Button) opened for 300, and Elon (Small Blind) 3-bet to 900. While a 3-bet into two players usually indicates strength, KQs is far too strong to fold in this short-handed dynamic. Shoving for 5075 (50 BBs) is an option, but it likely only gets called by hands that dominate me (AK, AA, KK, QQ) or are coin flips (JJ-99). By calling, I keep the pot manageable and use my hand's high post-flop playability. I am getting roughly 2.6:1 on a call (800 to win 2100 if Minni folds), which is sufficient given the equity of KQs against a standard 3-bet range in 3-max. I will call and evaluate the flop.

I opened to 300 on the button with 7♥9♦, but Elon 3-bet to 900 and Flash (BB) called, creating a multiway pot. 7‑9 offsuit is marginal — playable in position heads-up, but against a 3‑bet plus a caller it’s frequently dominated and has poor equity and playability. The pot is 2,100; calling 600 more (900 total) would give me 4.5:1 pot odds, requiring ~18% equity to break even. Versus a 3‑bettor’s value-weighted range and a calling BB range my realistic equity is likely below that, and 4‑betting with this hand is not sensible. I’ll fold and preserve my 13,575 stack for a better spot.

Flop board Kc 4c 2h is draw-heavy with flush draw potential and low coordinated cards, hitting BB flatting ranges (suited connectors, Ax suited, mid pairs). AToo holds Ac nut flush blocker, Ace-high with backdoor potential, but no made hand. Low SPR (~2:1 effective) after preflop 3bet/call; checking OOP invites valuebets from Kx/pairs/draws and forces fold equity loss. C-bet pot exploits Flash's capped range (unlikely sets/top pair top from BB call), applies max pressure with blocker, denies equity to draws. History shows Flash folds to aggression postflop (e.g., Hand 20, 22, 28). Pot-sized bet 2100 controls SPR, forces all-in decision; +EV bluff given fold equity >35% vs polarized calling range.

I have flopped top pair with a strong kicker (KQ) on a relatively dry board (K-4-2). Elon's pot-sized bet is very aggressive, especially following a pre-flop 3-bet, which suggests a polarized range of either a very strong hand (like AK, AA, or a set) or a high-equity draw/bluff. In a 3-handed tournament, folding top pair on this board would be overly tight and exploitable. Given the low Stack-to-Pot Ratio (SPR) of approximately 1.0 (after my call), I am effectively committed to the hand. Calling is preferable to raising here as it allows Elon to continue bluffing with hands like AQ or flush draws on the turn, whereas a shove only gets called by hands that have me beat.

Turn As pairs my Ac, giving me top pair (Aces) with a Ten kicker and continuing to block the nut club flush. The board is 4c Kc 2h As: still draw-heavy but the Ace likely misses many of Flash's flop calling range (suited connectors, mid-pairs, weaker Kx, missed flush/straight draws). Flash's BB flat-call vs 3bet OOP preflop and pot-sized flop call into my strong range suggests he has showdown value like KQo+, JTs+, pocket pairs 88-QQ, or stubborn draws. However, low SPR (~1:1 effective with his 2175 stack into 6300 pot) and my range advantage (3bet pre, pot cbet flop) mean thin value shoving maximizes EV: gets value from worse Ax (AJ-A9), underpairs, missed draws; fold equity from Kx without A (KQ, KT); protection vs draws. History shows Flash passive/calling station but folds to repeated aggression (Hands 20,22,29). Checking risks giving free card or facing shove with tough call OOP, better to deny equity and build pot. Shove 2175 ~1/3 pot sizing optimal for max pressure.

I am facing an all-in decision (or a bet that covers my remaining stack) on the turn. Pre-flop, I called a 3-bet with KQs, and on the flop (K-4-2), I had top pair with a strong kicker. I’m heads-up on the button with A♣2♠ and I’ve paired the deuce on a fairly dry Q♣9♥2♦ flop — bottom pair but with the Ace kicker. Facing a small 200 bet into an 800 pot (4:1 odds) on a 2d Qc 9h flop with 6h8h. Check. I have only bottom pair (pair of 2s) with an Ace kicker. On the turn board of 2d Qc 9h Tc, holding 6h8h for a live heart flush draw (9 outs) and gutshot straight draw to the 7 (4 outs, ~22% equity overall). I have A♥2♠ — bottom pair (pair of 2s) with the Ace kicker. I rivered two pair (Aces & Twos). Deep stacks (230bb effective) favor folding weak bluff-catchers to preserve stack for better spots; calling risks 1100 (~5bb) with poor EV long-term. Standard disciplined fold realizes 0 EV but avoids -EV hero call.

Facing a small continuation bet of 300 into a 1000 pot (~4.3:1 odds, requiring ~19% equity) on a dry Ac Ks Th rainbow board with top pair weak kicker (Kd7s) OOP vs Minni's wide button raising range.
