Flash opened to 1,275 from the small blind and now has only ~50 left. I hold 9♥6♠ — a weak, unsuited hand that is frequently dominated by a raiser’s range. Calling 1,175 to win the current ~1,375 pot gives me roughly 46% pot-equity requirement (1,175 / (1,375+1,175) ≈ 46%). 9-6o does not have that equity versus a reasonable shove/open range from the SB, and reraising to isolate would commit even more chips unnecessarily. With a healthy stack (15,125) and plenty of spots left, preserving chips is the better long-term play — fold.

I am holding 7-2 offsuit, which is widely considered the worst starting hand in Texas Hold'em. With a stack of 1425 chips (roughly 14 big blinds), I need to be selective about the hands I play to avoid bleeding chips. Entering the pot with such a weak hand from the button is not justifiable, even as a steal attempt, especially against opponents with significantly larger stacks who can easily call or re-raise. Folding is the optimal strategy here.

I’m on the button (small blind) heads-up with K♦J♠ — a strong, playable hand that performs well post‑flop and benefits from positional advantage. With ~150 BB effective I want to seize initiative rather than limp; calling 50 (completing to 100) is too passive and folding is overly tight. Elon’s big‑blind defending range will be wide, so a standard 3x BB open (300 total) gives fold equity, builds a pot with a hand that can extract value, and keeps post‑flop playability. Plan: raise to 300; if Elon 3‑bets large I’ll fold or re‑evaluate by sizing, if he calls I’ll use position to c‑bet and realize equity.

AJo is a strong hand from the Big Blind facing a Small Blind open-raise to 3BB. It has excellent equity against Minni's likely wide SB raising range, which from history includes many speculative hands. AJ blocks AA and AK, and postflop playability is good heads-up with position. Raising takes initiative, isolates if Flash wakes up (unlikely), and applies pressure on Minni who folds often to 3-bets (e.g., Hand 32). Sizing to 900 (3x her raise) is standard pot-building size with deep stacks. Calling is fine but gives her cheap equity realization.

I opened to 300 on the button with K♦J♠ and Elon 3‑bet to 900 (standard size). With ~150BB effective KJ is a clear flat here — it plays well postflop in position and is not a hand I want to 4‑bet bluff or fold face‑up to a normal 3‑bet. Calling 600 into the 1200 pot gives ~33% break‑even odds; against Elon's likely mixed 3‑bet range I have reasonable equity and positional advantage. Plan: call to 900, see the flop, c‑bet/pressure favorable boards (K‑X, dynamic draws) and proceed cautiously on A‑high boards or versus heavy action; fold to very large 4‑bets/All‑ins.

Flop (A♥7♦5♠) I’m in position with K♦J♠ after calling Elon's 3‑bet pre. I have no pair and the Ace on board is very likely to be in a 3‑bettor’s range, so a lead c‑bet is a bluff into a range that contains many Ax and strong hands — not ideal. I want pot control and the informational advantage of acting last. Check to see how Elon reacts: call small c‑bets to realize equity and float/capitalize on favorable turn cards (K/J or safe running cards), but fold to large overbets or heavy aggression. If Elon checks back I get a free turn to either take the pot or fire a delayed bet on a safe card.

Top pair top kicker (Aces with Jack kicker) on a dry Ace-high flop (Ah7d5s). This is an excellent spot for value betting as the board favors my range significantly after my pre-flop 3-bet and her check. Minni's SB calling range includes Ax worse than AJ (like A-rag, suited aces), mid pairs, suited connectors/draws that missed. Straight draws (e.g., 68, 46) are possible but weak; no strong draws completed. Her check indicates weakness or caution, unlikely to have AA/AK given pre-flop action. Betting 2/3 pot (1200 into 1800) builds the pot, denies equity to draws, and gets value from her worse hands that will call. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards/draws or letting her bluff. Deep stacks allow multi-street value.

Call 1200. I opened pre and flat the 3‑bet to 900 with K♦J♠; on A♥7♦5♠ I have no pair but two overcards and the button (position). Elon’s 1200 c‑bet into ~3000 (~40%) is a standard, relatively small continuation from a wide 3‑bet range that includes many missed hands and some Ax combos. Pot control + positional advantage favor a call to realize equity and see a turn rather than bluff‑raising into a 3‑bettor or folding a playable hand for cheap. I’ll call to 1200, see the turn, fold to heavy turn aggression, and look to extract value or raise only if I pair (K/J) or pick up a very favorable runout.

Check. The turn K♥ pairs my K♦ for a decent but vulnerable second pair — the board still has an A and Elon was the pre‑flop 3‑better and c‑betted the flop, so his continuing range contains many A‑x and some two‑pair/AK combos that still beat me. Betting here would bloated the pot and often put me in a commit/fold spot versus raises; in position I prefer pot control and to give myself the option to call a small/medium turn bet and fold to heavy aggression. If Elon checks back I can re‑evaluate the river for value/bluff opportunities; if he bets small I’ll call to realize equity, and I’ll fold to large overbets/raises that signal strong Ax or two‑pair.

Turn board brings the Kh, pairing the board slightly with overcards but not drastically changing the texture. I still hold top pair top kicker (AsJd), which remains very strong on Ah7d5sKh. The king brings heart flush draws into play (two hearts now) and possible Kx hands (KQ, KT, suited kings), but Minni's SB check-call range vs my 3-bet and flop c-bet is unlikely to include many strong kings: AK would often 4-bet pre, and weaker Kx might have folded flop. More likely: weaker Ax (A8-A10, suited), 77/55/sets (unlikely given check), mid pairs (88-JJ, QQ), stubborn draws (hearts, 68 str8 draw). Her check suggests no monster and invites a bet. Deep stacks (my 21k, her 13k) warrant continuation betting for thin value from worse Ax/pairs and protection vs draws/improves. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing her to rep the K. Bet 2/3 pot (2800 into 4200) balances value/protection, sizes up from flop, and keeps pressure on her folding range. Pot odds favorable for calls from worse.

Call 2800 (total committed 4900). I’ve paired the King (K♦J♠ on A♥7♦5♠K♥) and I’m in position — a realizable, but vulnerable, one‑pair hand. Pot odds on a 2800 call into a 7000 pot require ~28.6% equity, which Kx has versus a 3‑better’s mixed range that contains many non‑Ace broadways and occasional bluffs in addition to Ax value combos. Elon’s line (preflop 3‑bet, small flop c‑bet, moderate turn stab) is consistent with both value (AK/Ax, 77/55) and light barrels; raising here would bloat the pot and force marginal decisions when I’m likely dominated by Ax and sometimes Kx with better kickers. Calling keeps the pot controlled, preserves post‑flop leverage in position, and lets me fold to large river aggression or extract value on a safe river or a check back. If Elon over‑pressures (large raise/shove) I will re‑evaluate and likely fold absent a clear bluff; if he checks river I’ll look for a small value bet or showdown.

Board A♥7♦5♠K♥4♥ — I hold K♦J♠: top pair, decent but vulnerable. Villain was the preflop 3‑better and fired flop then barreled the turn, a line heavily weighted to Ax (AK/A7/A5), sets or two‑pair — many hands that still beat my K. The river 4♥ completes a 3‑heart board but only makes a flush if the opponent holds two hearts (a relatively small subset of his 3‑bet range). Betting here will mostly get called by better hands and fold only the few worse hands; it needlessly bloats a large pot when I’m likely behind. With ~10k behind and a 9.8k pot I prefer pot control and a showdown. I’ll check to avoid committing more chips; if he fires a small/medium river bet I can call for pot control or fold to a large polarizing raise.

River 4h completes obvious heart flushes (three hearts on board) and gutshot str8s like 68 (4-5-6-7-8 straight), plus pairs the low end for two-pair combos (A4, 54, 74 unlikely). However, Minni's passive check-check-call-call line from SB vs my 3bet + double cbet is polarized weak-strong but weighted heavily toward medium strength: weaker Ax (A10-A2, suited/off), mid pairs (88-QQ, trapped sets unlikely preflop), turned Kx (KQs/KTs but weak kicker/call flop), stubborn draws that missed. Strong flushes (two+ hearts) would often donk or raise earlier streets given pot investment; her consistent checking suggests no nuts or monster, likely capping at showdown value worse than AJ (e.g., A8, 77, 55). AJs TPTK blocks AA/AK, crushes her typical Ax/pair calling range. Pot 9800, her stack 10225 (effective ~10k), mine deep. Value bet ~60% pot (6000) extracts from worse (A-rag calls 50-70% time per villain tendencies), forces folds from missed draws/bluffs/air, builds pot maximally without overbetting into her stack. Checking misses massive value from her sticky check-call history (e.g., Hands 11,20,22,28,33,34,37) and risks free showdown. Sizing correct: pot-committing her, +EV.

Call 6000. I have top pair (K♦) with a decent but vulnerable J kicker on A♥ 7♦ 5♠ K♥ 4♥. Elon was the pre‑flop 3‑better and has barreled flop and turn, a line weighted toward Ax (AK/A7/A5), sets (77/55) and other value hands — but also contains some missed broadway/backdoor bluffs. The river bet is 6000 into a 15800 pot, so I’m getting ~27.5% break‑even odds (6000 / (15800+6000) ≈ 0.275). That price makes a call reasonable: my hand has meaningful showdown value and beats the bluffs and weaker Kx combos that Elon will sometimes use to barrel. Folding concedes too much equity given the pot odds; raising all‑in is a poor play because I’m often racing vs Ax/two‑pair/sets and I would be building a pot when I’m frequently behind. I’ll call to see showdown — if Elon shows a thin value or bluff I’ll win a big pot; if he has a clear two‑pair/set/AK I lose a priced decision but preserve stack structure for future hands.

Hole cards T♦2♥ are very weak unsuited. The pot is only the blinds (150) and calling 100 into a multiway pot and then playing postflop against a very deep, frequently aggressive small blind (Elon, 34,300) and a short-stacked big blind (Flash, 1,325) is negative EV. Raising to 200 to try to steal risks an immediate shove/call from the short stack or a 3-bet from Elon, leaving me in a poor spot with a garbage hand. I preserve my ~42bb stack for better situations and fold this marginal holding.

Pocket Queens (QQ) is a premium hand, one of the best starting hands in NLHE. With the button (Minni) folding, I'm facing only the Big Blind (Flash) who has a short stack of 1325 (~13BB). Raising from the small blind after a fold is standard to build the pot, apply pressure on the short stack, and isolate if called. Standard sizing is 3x the BB (300 total), which is aggressive but appropriate given my deep stack (343BB) and the short BB. QQ crushes typical calling/shoving ranges from BB. No need to limp or min-raise; 300 provides value and fold equity.

I am in the Big Blind with 6d 5s and a short stack of 1,325 chips (approx. 13 BB). Elon, who has a massive chip lead, raised from the Small Blind to 300. While I would have position post-flop, 65o is a very weak hand to defend with such a shallow stack. Calling 200 more represents about 15% of my remaining chips, and I won't have the implied odds to play a speculative hand. It is better to fold and wait for a stronger hand to commit my remaining chips.

Preflop on the button with K2o. Pot is 150 (likely SB folded their 50, BB posted 100 and checked), facing only the BB who checked, indicating a weak range. Excellent position and stealing opportunity with dead money in the pot. K2o has a king blocker, decent high card, and plays well postflop in position against a single opponent. Raising steals the pot often or builds it with initiative. Standard size around 2.5-3x BB. Flash is short-stacked at ~13BB but appears to have folded. Minni has 41BB and checked BB, wide folding range to button raise.

I am holding 8d 4h in the Small Blind. This is a very weak hand. My stack size is approximately 12.75 big blinds, making me the short stack at the table. While I am looking for an opportunity to double up, 84o is not a hand worth defending or shoving with, especially out of position against a massive chip leader who opened from the button. I will preserve my remaining chips and wait for a better spot.

I have AdQc (AQo) in the big blind facing Elon’s button open to 250. Effective stack ~4,125 (~41bb). AQo is a strong hand here and performs well as a 3-bet: it has good equity versus a wide BTN opening range and blocks many of Elon’s strongest Ace/Queen combos. With Flash folded this is effectively heads-up — I want to isolate and take the initiative while I have fold equity and a hand that plays well postflop. A standard 3-bet sizing of ~3x the open gives meaningful pressure without overcommitting (250×3 ≈ 750). Plan: if he calls I’ll play the flop OOP but with a high-card/hand-strength advantage; if he 4-bets very large I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to an extreme shove). Therefore I 3-bet to 750.

Facing a 3-bet to 750 (3x my open) from the BB with K2o offsuit on the button. K2o has a king blocker but is otherwise weak, with poor playability postflop: not connected, low kicker prone to domination by better kings, and little straight/flush potential. Minni's stack is ~35BB, so she can continue aggressively postflop. Pot odds are decent (~2:1 effective after rake, but calling commits ~10% of pot OOP for her postflop), but equity against a polarized 3-bet range (value like QQ+, AK or bluffs) is marginal (~30-40% at best). 4-betting bluff risks her shoving her stack with better hands, and my deep stack prefers avoiding variance with trash. Folding preserves stack for better spots; the initial raise stole blinds often in prior hands, but this defense is standard strength.

I am currently the short stack with approximately 12.75 big blinds. In a 3-handed game, King-high suited (Kc 3c) is a strong hand to open from the button. Given my stack size, a standard raise-fold strategy is inefficient; instead, an all-in shove is the mathematically optimal move to maximize fold equity and ensure I realize all my equity if called. This pressure is especially important against the chip leader (Elon), who can afford to call wide, and the small blind (Minni), who is also relatively short compared to Elon.

Flash has shoved/raised to 1,275 from the button and is effectively all‑in. Pot is 1,425; I must call 1,225 more (about 12.25 BB) from my 4,475 stack. Calling requires roughly 46% equity to break even heads‑up (1,225 / (1,425+1,225) ≈ 46.2%), but 4♥2♥ has only ~30% (or worse if Flash’s shove is not totally random) and falls further if Elon (deep BB) also calls. A min‑raise to 2,450 to try to isolate would commit a large chunk of my stack and will likely just get called by Elon. With a weak, often‑dominated hand and plenty of tournament left, preserving chips is correct — fold.

74o is a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Facing a large button raise to 12.75BB from Flash, who has shown aggression with similar oversized raises (e.g., Hand 39). Pot odds are poor (calling 1175 into 1425, ~55% equity needed), and my equity against a button opening range is <30%. No reason to speculate or bluff-raise OOP in BB. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots in tournament.

Three-handed on the button with J♦T♣ is a clear open-raise spot. I have ~44bb, so I want to take the initiative and steal the blinds / play position postflop. The BB is short ( ~13bb ) and will often fold or shove; Elon in the SB is very deep and can defend wide, but I prefer opening to avoid giving them the initiative. I’ll raise to 300 (≈3x the big blind) — big enough to apply pressure and build a pot with position, but small enough to keep postflop playability. If the short-stacked BB shoves I’ll evaluate but I can comfortably call given my stack and JTo’s reasonable equity vs a wide shove; if the SB 3-bets large I can fold and preserve my stack.

9d3s is an extremely weak offsuit hand with no connectivity, suited potential, or high card value. Facing a button raise to 300 (needing 250 more into 450 pot), getting under 2:1 with position disadvantage postflop. No reason to call or raise; Flash in BB may defend, but folding preserves stack for better spots. Standard fold.

I am in the Big Blind with 2h 7d, which is statistically the weakest hand in poker. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. My stack is very short (1325 chips, 13.25 BB), but defending with this hand is not profitable, especially out of position. Folding is the only viable option to preserve my remaining chips for a better opportunity.

3-handed on the button with TcJc, a strong suited connector with excellent playability and implied odds. Pot is 150 with 100 to call the BB (likely SB folded and BB checked or similar, given no prior actions listed and my position). Raising is standard here to steal the blinds, build the pot, or play heads-up in position against the BB. My massive stack (34k) vs opponents' shorter stacks (Flash 12.75BB, Minni 45BB) allows aggression without risk. A 3x open to 300 is balanced size, consistent with prior opens, denying equity to worse hands and setting up postflop control.

I am currently the short stack with only 12.75 big blinds (1275 chips). While 9c 3c is suited, it is an extremely weak hand to defend from the Small Blind against a raise from the chip leader, Elon, who has been consistently aggressive. Committing more chips here with such a low-equity hand would leave me even more vulnerable. I will wait for a better spot to commit my remaining chips to double up.

I’m in the big blind facing a button open to 300 with Q♥2♣. This is a weak, easily dominated offsuit queen, but the price to defend is small (200 more, 300 total committed including my blind) relative to my 4525 stack (~45bb). Elon has a very deep stack and will call/re-raise wide, so a 3-bet bluff here is unattractive — I’d be OOP and likely face calls or a shove with better equity. Calling keeps the pot manageable, preserves fold equity postflop when I hit, and lets me see a cheap flop to realize equity. Plan: call 300; on dry, unfavorable flops I’ll check and fold to heavy action, while I can probe/c-bet when I pick up real equity or favorable runouts.

Check. I have Qh2c on 8d-4h-9s — no pair, no real draw (only 3 queens + 3 deuces = 6 outs, ~24% to pair by river). The pot is 650 and Elon is very deep and likely to c-bet wide, so leading as a bluff OOP is unattractive — I’ll get called or raised by better hands and don’t want to build the pot with almost no equity. Checking preserves pot control and lets me fold to meaningful aggression. If Elon bets a very small probe (≈200 or less) I may call since the break-even call threshold is roughly that (200–210); otherwise I’ll fold and look for a better spot. If he checks back I’ll reassess on the turn.

Preflop raiser on a flop that connects decently with my button opening range: 8d-4h-9s gives me an open-ended straight draw with TcJc (7 or Q completes 7-8-9-T-J or 8-9-T-J-Q), 8 outs (~32% equity), backdoor flush draw, and good playability. Checked to me in position heads-up vs Minni's BB calling range (which is wide based on history of calling raises with marginal hands). C-bet sizing at ~60% pot (400 into 650) balances value from top pair-ish hands in my range, semi-bluffs like this draw, and protection while denying her equity on overcards/draws. My deep stack (337BB effective) vs her 43BB allows pressure without commitment; fold equity high given her shorter stack and history of folding to aggression postflop when behind (e.g., Hand 20, 29). Checking risks giving free cards to her draws/OESD and loses value when ahead.

I called preflop to see a cheap flop, but on 8♦-4♥-9♠ with Q♥2♣ I have no pair and only ~6 outs (~24% to hit by the river). Elon bets 400 into the 650 pot (pot now 1050). A call of 400 requires roughly 27.6% equity to break even (400 / (1050+400)), which is more than my realistic equity vs his c-bet range. I’m out of position against a very deep opponent, and a raise/bluff OOP is unattractive here. Preserving my ~43bb stack and waiting for a better spot is the best play, so I fold.

I am on the button with a very short stack of 12.75 big blinds. Nine-four suited is a weak hand that does not warrant an open-raise at this stack depth, especially with Elon (a massive stack) and Minni still to act. Folding is the correct play to preserve my remaining chips for a stronger hand or a more viable shove opportunity.

I have A♥ J♥ in the small blind and the button (Flash) folded — heads-up vs Elon in the big blind (very deep ~343 BB). AJs is a strong, playable raising hand; limping is weak. I want initiative and fold equity while keeping postflop maneuverability with ~42 BB effective. A standard 2.5–3x open accomplishes that and prices out some weaker hands; a min-raise to 200 is too small. I’ll raise to 300 total (putting pressure on the BB). If Elon calls I’ll continuation-bet favorable flops; if he 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and range.

Facing a standard small raise from the SB after BTN folds, my Ac2c is a strong defensive hand in the BB. Suited ace blocks AA/AK/AQ and has excellent playability with flush and straight potential. SB opening range is very wide (any ace, suited connectors, broadway, pairs). I have ~45% equity vs a typical SB range. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into 400 (2:1). Postflop OOP is fine with this hand as it flops well and can realize equity. No need to fold or 3-bet; calling maximizes EV while controlling pot size early.

I have A♥ J♥ on J♦ 7♦ 8♣ — top pair with the best kicker. I’m out of position and Elon defended a wide preflop calling range, which contains many worse jacks (KJ/QJ), weaker pairs, and plenty of straight/diamond draws (notably T9 straights and two-diamond backdoor/flush draws). Checking lets him see free cards and realize equity; a value/protection c-bet is preferable. I’ll bet ~2/3 pot (400 into 600) to extract from worse one-pair hands, charge the draws, and define his range. If he raises big I’ll re-evaluate (large raises often represent straights/sets here); if he simply calls I can continue with pot control on later streets.

Facing a standard continuation bet of 400 into 1000 from Minni's wide SB opening range on a Jd7d8c flop. My Ac2c holds the nut club flush draw (35% equity standalone), ace-high overcard that blocks strong Ax hands like AK/AQ/AT, and decent playability. Pot odds are excellent (calling 400 to win 1000 requires ~28.6% equity, but I have far more vs her polarized c-bet range which includes bluffs, weak pairs, and draws). Raising risks folding out better but drawing hands or getting called by worse; calling keeps pot small OOP, realizes equity on later streets, and allows bluff-catching with position post-turn if needed. Minni's stack (3625) is covered, no ICM pressure in mid-tournament. Folding is -EV given equity and price.

Check. I have top pair with the best kicker (A♣ J♣) but I’m out of position and the turn paired the board (J♦ 7♦ 8♣ → 8♥). Elon called a large (2/3 pot) c‑bet on the flop, so his continuing range contains a lot of strong hands and made combinations that get much stronger on this turn: 9‑10 already had straights on the flop, 7‑8/77/88 can now have full houses or quads, and many two‑pair/set combos will happily check‑raise or trap. Betting here would mostly extract from a narrower set of worse Jx hands while exposing me to large check‑raises or overbets OOP; with pot = 1400 and my stack 3625 I don’t want to inflate the pot with just one pair. Checking controls the pot, keeps bluffs in his range, and lets me reevaluate on the river (call small/medium river bets for showdown, fold or pot‑commit only to very strong river action representing full houses/quads).

Minni (SB) checked the turn after betting the flop, checking it to me (BB OOP). Board is Jd7d8c8h: paired eights, diamond draw, possible straight draws (T9, 56, 95). My Ac2c holds the nut club flush draw (9 outs, ~36% to nut flush on river), ace-high blocker to strong Ax hands, no made hand. Her flop bet-check turn line is consistent with weak pairs (77, 99-TT?), Jx, missed draws, or traps with sets/straights. Equity remains strong (~35-45% vs her range). Pot odds to see river are infinite (to call 0). Betting risks bloating pot OOP with a draw, facing raises from made hands or floats; her check likely weakness, but I prefer realizing equity cheaply OOP rather than semi-bluffing into potential monsters or getting called by showdown value. Checking back controls pot size, avoids difficult river spots OOP, and maximizes EV by getting there for free ~36% of the time while showdowning if needed. Consistent with prior passive OOP strategy to realize equity without overcommitting deepstacked.

Check. The river A♠ gives me top two pair (A♠/A♥ and J♦/J♥) but I’m out of position and the board is paired (8c/8h), so there are believable full‑house/quads combos (77, 88, JJ, or combos with an Ace) that can beat me. Elon defended a wide calling range preflop, called my 2/3 flop c‑bet, then checked the turn — that line looks like a lot of marginal Jx/KJ/QJ, some straights (T9), and missed draws, with some slower strong hands mixed in. I checked the turn to control the pot and keep bluffs in his range; betting the river would extract from worse hands but also risks getting check‑raised/overbet by the few full‑house/quads combos and committing a large portion of my ~3625 stack to a monster. Checking down realizes equity against his wide continuing range and lets me call small/medium river bets but fold to a very large raise or shove that credibly represents a full house. If he checks behind I get to showdown with a very strong but non‑invulnerable hand.

I have A♥J♥ — river gives me two pair (Aces & Jacks) on J♦7♦8♣8♥A♠. Elon defended a wide range preflop, called a 2/3-pot c‑bet on the flop, then checked the turn; that line contains many worse Jx hands, missed draws, some straights (T9) and a smaller number of full‑house/quads combos (pocket JJ/77/88/AA and mixed 8‑X combos like J8/A8/78). I have bottom pair (Deuces) on a board of 6d-8d-8h-Ah-2h, giving me two pair: Eights and Deuces with an Ace kicker. Post-flop on a paired 5c5s4d board, which is very dry and favors my button preflop raising range (high cards, broadways, pairs) over Minni's wide BB defending range (many weak hands, low pairs, suited junk). I’m in the big blind with Qc8d on 5c 5s 4d. I checked the flop and Elon (BTN) c-bet 400 into a pot that was ~650 (pot now 1050); calling 400 to win the pot (final pot ≈1450) requires ~27.6% equity.
